text.title: E-commerce Checkout BA Pack Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every E-commerce Checkout BA Pack application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Document PCI-DSS scope: which components touch card data (PAN), how SAQ level is determined, where tokenization happens. Vague PCI scope kills audits.
rule.rationale: PCI scope ambiguity is the #1 audit failure; explicit scope diagram is the only safe artefact.

rule.statement: Build a VAT decision table: ship-to country × seller-VAT-registration → rate + invoice format. EU OSS, UK VAT post-Brexit, US sales tax nexus each have distinct rules.
rule.rationale: Tax errors are post-launch lawsuits; pre-launch table prevents most of them.

rule.statement: Fraud-rule decision table: signals (BIN country, AVS, CVV, IP geo, basket value) × thresholds → action (approve, 3DS, manual review, block).
rule.rationale: Fraud thresholds are domain-specific; explicit table is reviewable + auditable + tunable.

rule.statement: Abandonment state machine: cart-open → email-1 (1h) → email-2 (24h) → email-3 (72h) → close. Consent + suppression rules per region.
rule.rationale: Most teams underspec the suppression rules; result is GDPR violations + Spamhaus listings.

rule.statement: Returns flow: RMA request → label generation → receive → refund decision → refund execution → restock. Each step has SLA + responsible system.
rule.rationale: Returns are 8-30% of e-commerce volume; under-specified returns flow is the #1 customer-service ticket source.
